calendar.matters:
Petition And Heggstad Petition To Transfer Real Property Into Trust Probate Code Section 17200
Off Calendar Mar-15-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Bridgman for and with (trustee & executor) Michele Marsh.

The Court stated that the petition is asking the court to order property into a trust, that the paperwork did not get done, that the existence of a Pour-Over will is irrelevant, that executed intent does not satisfy the requirement and asked counsel what he has to show that the property should be in the trust. Mr. Bridgman stated that the intent of the party is the important thing for Heggstad. The Court stated that it examines the paperwork regarding intent and that if it shows up on Schedule A it shows intent. Mr. Bridgman stated that the other properties were deeded into the trust, that it shows her intent, that if she had not had the stroke it would have happened and that it seems terrible to have to go through Probate to get the property into the trust. The Court stated that it does not engage in posthumous estate planning and that if there are no additional documents the Heggstad petition to transfer real property into the trust is denied.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos & Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)
